words.def: in order to help somebody
words.example: He began a petition in behalf of the farmers.

words.def: loved very much
words.example: in memory of our dearly beloved son, John

words.def: a long seat for two or more people, usually made of wood
words.example: a park bench

words.def: something that can be measured and used as a standard that other things can be compared with
words.example: Tests at the age of seven provide a benchmark against which the child's progress at school can be measured.

words.def: in or to a lower position than somebody/something; under somebody/something
words.example: They found the body buried beneath a pile of leaves.

words.def: a person who gains as a result of something
words.example: Who will be the main beneficiary of the cuts in income tax?

words.def: to give information about somebody/something to an enemy
words.example: betray somebody/something, He was offered money to betray his colleagues.

words.def: to tie somebody/something with rope, string, etc. so that they/it cannot move or are held together strongly
words.example: bind somebody/something to something, She was bound to a chair.

words.def: the story of a person’s life written by somebody else; this type of writing
words.example: Boswell’s biography of Johnson

words.def: a senior priest in charge of the work of the Church in a city or district
words.example: the Bishop of Oxford

words.def: very strange or unusual
words.example: a bizarre situation/incident/story

words.def: the flat part of a knife, tool or machine, which has a sharp edge or edges for cutting
words.example: The machine comes with a plastic guard over the blade to protect the operator.

words.def: an explosion or a powerful movement of air caused by an explosion
words.example: a bomb blast

words.def: to lose blood, especially from a wound or an injury
words.example: My finger's bleeding.

words.def: a mixture of different types of the same thing
words.example: a blend of tea

words.def: to ask God to protect somebody/something
words.example: They brought the children to Jesus and he blessed them.

words.def: God’s help and protection, or a prayer asking for this
words.example: to pray for God’s blessing

words.def: to talk in a way that shows you are too proud of something that you have or can do
words.example: I don't want to boast, but I can actually speak six languages.

words.def: an extra amount of money that is added to a payment, especially to somebody’s wages or salary as a reward
words.example: a £100 Christmas bonus

words.def: a sudden increase in trade and economic activity; a period of wealth and success
words.example: Living standards improved rapidly during the post-war boom.

words.def: if something bounces or you bounce it, it moves quickly away from a surface it has just hit or you make it do this
words.example: The ball bounced twice before he could reach it.

words.def: a real or imagined line that marks the limits or edges of something and separates it from other things or places; a dividing line
words.example: After the war the national boundaries were redrawn.

words.def: the act of bending your head or the upper part of your body forward in order to say hello or goodbye to somebody or to show respect
words.example: She gave a slight bow of her head in greeting.

words.def: a failure to do something that must be done by law
words.example: a breach of contract/copyright/warranty

words.def: an occasion when a vehicle or machine stops working
words.example: a breakdown on the motorway

words.def: an important development that may lead to an agreement or achievement
words.example: to make/achieve a breakthrough

words.def: a particular type of animal that has been developed by people in a certain way, especially a type of dog, cat or farm animal
words.example: Labradors and other large breeds of dog

words.def: a way of connecting to the internet that allows you to receive information, including pictures, etc., very quickly and that is always active (so that the user does not have to connect each time)
words.example: plans to provide rural areas with fast broadband

words.def: a computer program that lets you look at or read documents on the World Wide Web
words.example: What do you use as your default browser?

words.def: violent and cruel
words.example: a brutal attack/murder/rape/killing

words.def: a US, Australian or New Zealand dollar; a South African rand; an Indian rupee
words.example: They cost ten bucks.

words.def: a friend
words.example: an old college buddy of mine

words.def: a thing or person that reduces a shock or protects somebody/something against difficulties
words.example: buffer against something, Support from family and friends acts as a buffer against stress.

words.def: the main part of something; most of something
words.example: The bulk of the population lives in cities.

words.def: a duty, responsibility, etc. that causes worry, difficulty or hard work
words.example: to bear/carry/ease/reduce/share the burden

words.def: the system of official rules and ways of doing things that a government or an organization has, especially when these seem to be too complicated
words.example: unnecessary/excessive bureaucracy

words.def: the act or ceremony of burying a dead body
words.example: a burial place/mound/site

words.def: to break open or apart, especially because of pressure from inside; to make something break in this way
words.example: That balloon will burst if you blow it up any more.

words.def: a group of senior members of a government that is responsible for advising and deciding on government policy
words.example: a cabinet meeting

words.def: the act or process of using numbers to find out an amount
words.example: Cathy did a rough calculation.

words.def: a strong heavy rough material used for making tents, sails, etc. and by artists for painting on
words.example: tents made from heavy canvas

words.def: the ability or qualities necessary to do something
words.example: Age affects the range of a person's capabilities.

words.def: an economic system in which a country’s businesses and industry are controlled and run for profit by private owners rather than by the government
words.example: the growth of industrial capitalism in the West

words.def: based on the principles of capitalism
words.example: a capitalist society/system/economy

words.def: the goods carried in a ship, an aircraft or a motor vehicle
words.example: The tanker began to spill its cargo of oil.

words.def: a separate section of a train for carrying passengers
words.example: a railway carriage

words.def: to make objects, patterns, etc. by cutting away material from a piece of wood or stone, or another hard material
words.example: carve something, a carved doorway

words.def: a public building or room where people play gambling games for money
words.example: a new hotel and casino in Las Vegas

words.def: a person who is killed or injured in war or in an accident
words.example: Our primary objective is reducing road casualties.

words.def: a complete list of items, for example of things that people can look at or buy
words.example: a mail-order catalogue (= a book showing goods for sale to be sent to people’s homes)

words.def: to provide food and drinks for a social event
words.example: (British English), cater for somebody/something, Most of our work now involves catering for weddings.

words.def: cows and bulls that are kept as farm animals for their milk or meat
words.example: a herd of cattle

words.def: care that you take in order to avoid danger or mistakes; the fact of not taking any risks
words.example: extreme/great caution

words.def: being careful about what you say or do, especially to avoid danger or mistakes; not taking any risks
words.example: The government has been cautious in its response to the report.

words.def: to stop happening or existing; to stop something from happening or existing
words.example: Welfare payments cease as soon as an individual starts a job.

words.def: an area of land used for burying dead people, especially one that is not next to a church
words.example: He was buried in a private cemetery.

words.def: a hall in a public building that is used for formal meetings
words.example: The members left the council chamber.

words.def: a complete lack of order
words.example: economic/political/domestic chaos

words.def: to be typical of a person, place or thing
words.example: the rolling hills that characterize this part of England

words.def: the power of pleasing or attracting people
words.example: He was a man of great charm.

words.def: a written statement describing the rights that a particular group of people should have
words.example: the European Social Charter of workers’ rights

words.def: lasting for a long time; difficult to cure
words.example: chronic bronchitis/arthritis/asthma

words.def: a thick, solid piece that has been cut or broken off something
words.example: a chunk of cheese/masonry

words.def: when a liquid, gas or air circulates or is circulated, it moves continuously around a place or system
words.example: The condition prevents the blood from circulating freely.

words.def: the movement of blood around the body
words.example: Regular exercise will improve blood circulation.

words.def: the legal right to belong to a particular country
words.example: They were granted full French citizenship.

words.def: officially connected with a town or city
words.example: civic buildings/leaders

words.def: connected with people who are not members of the armed forces or the police
words.example: He left the army and returned to civilian life.

words.def: the quality of being expressed clearly
words.example: a lack of clarity in the law

words.def: a short fight between two groups of people
words.example: Clashes broke out between police and demonstrators.

words.def: the act or process of putting people or things into a group or class (= of classifying them)
words.example: a style of music that defies classification (= is like no other)

words.def: to hold on tightly to somebody/something
words.example: cling to somebody/something, survivors clinging to a raft

words.def: relating to the examination and treatment of patients and their illnesses
words.example: clinical research (= done on patients, not just considering theory)

words.def: the situation when a factory, school, hospital, etc. shuts permanently
words.example: factory closures

words.def: a group of things of the same type that grow or appear close together
words.example: The telescope is focused on a dense cluster of stars at the edge of the galaxy.

words.def: a government formed by two or more political parties working together
words.example: The two parties have formed a coalition.

words.def: of or near a coast
words.example: coastal waters/resorts/scenery

words.def: a drink usually made from a mixture of one or more spirits (= strong alcoholic drinks) and fruit juice. It can also be made without alcohol.
words.example: a cocktail bar/cabinet/lounge/shaker

words.def: connected with mental processes of understanding
words.example: a child’s cognitive development

words.def: to take place at the same time
words.example: It's a pity our trips to New York don't coincide.

words.def: to work together with somebody in order to produce or achieve something
words.example: Researchers around the world are collaborating to develop a new vaccine.

words.def: the act of working with another person or group of people to create or produce something
words.example: It was a collaboration that produced extremely useful results.

words.def: done or shared by all members of a group of people; involving a whole group or society
words.example: collective leadership/decision-making/responsibility

words.def: an accident in which two vehicles or people crash into each other
words.example: collision between A and B, a collision between two trains

words.def: connected with or belonging to a country that controls another country
words.example: a colonial power

words.def: a journalist who writes regular articles for a newspaper or magazine
words.example: a newspaper columnist

words.def: fighting or a fight, especially during a time of war
words.example: in combat, He was killed in combat.

words.def: to begin to happen; to begin something
words.example: The meeting is scheduled to commence at noon.

words.def: a spoken description of an event that is given while it is happening, especially on the radio or television
words.example: a sports commentary

words.def: a person who describes an event while it is happening, especially on television or radio
words.example: a television/sports commentator

words.def: trade, especially between countries; the buying and selling of goods and services
words.example: Leaders of industry and commerce met at the summit in Paris.

words.def: a member of a commission (= an official group of people who are responsible for controlling something or finding out about something)
words.example: the Church Commissioners (= the group of people responsible for controlling the financial affairs of the Church of England)

words.def: a product or a raw material that can be bought and sold
words.example: rice, flour and other basic commodities

words.def: connected with communism
words.example: communist ideology

words.def: a person or an animal that travels with you or spends a lot of time with you
words.example: travelling companions

words.def: similar to somebody/something else and able to be compared
words.example: A comparable house in the south of the city would cost twice as much.

words.def: a strong feeling of sympathy for people or animals who are suffering and a desire to help them
words.example: to feel/show compassion

words.def: to force somebody to do something; to make something necessary
words.example: compel somebody to do something, The law can compel fathers to make regular payments for their children.

words.def: that makes you pay attention to it because it is so interesting and exciting
words.example: Her latest book makes compelling reading.

words.def: to provide something good to balance or reduce the bad effects of damage, loss, etc.
words.example: Nothing can compensate for the loss of a loved one.

words.def: something, especially money, that somebody gives you because they have hurt you, or damaged something that you own; the act of giving this to somebody
words.example: to claim/award/receive compensation

words.def: the ability to do something well
words.example: to gain a high level of competence in English

words.def: having enough skill or knowledge to do something well or to the necessary standard
words.example: He's very competent in his work.

words.def: to produce a book, list, report, etc. by bringing together different items, articles, songs, etc.; to collect information in order to produce a book, list, etc.
words.example: We are trying to compile a list of suitable people for the job.

words.def: to add to something in a way that improves it or makes it more attractive
words.example: The excellent menu is complemented by a good wine list.

words.def: the state of being formed of many parts; the state of being difficult to understand
words.example: the increasing complexity of modern telecommunication systems

words.def: the practice of obeying rules or requests made by people in authority
words.example: compliance (with something), procedures that must be followed to ensure full compliance with the law

words.def: a thing that makes a situation more complicated or difficult
words.example: The bad weather added a further complication to our journey.

words.def: to obey a rule, an order, etc.; to meet particular standards
words.example: They refused to comply with the UN resolution.

words.def: the different parts that something is made of; the way in which the different parts are organized
words.example: the chemical composition of the soil

words.def: an agreement made between two people or groups in which each side gives up some of the things they want so that both sides are happy at the end
words.example: After lengthy talks the two sides finally reached a compromise.

words.def: to calculate something
words.example: The losses were computed at £5 million.

words.def: to hide somebody/something
words.example: conceal somebody/something, The paintings were concealed beneath a thick layer of plaster.

words.def: to admit that something is true, logical, etc. after first denying it or resisting it
words.example: + speech, ‘Not bad,’ she conceded grudgingly.

words.def: to form an idea, a plan, etc. in your mind
words.example: He conceived the idea of transforming the old power station into an arts centre.

words.def: an understanding or a belief of what something/somebody is or what something/somebody should be
words.example: conception of something, Marx’s conception of social justice

words.def: something that you allow or do, or allow somebody to have, in order to end an argument or to make a situation less difficult
words.example: The firm will be forced to make concessions if it wants to avoid a strike.

words.def: to say very strongly that you think something is bad, usually for moral reasons
words.example: condemn somebody/something, The government issued a statement condemning the killings.

words.def: to discuss something with somebody, in order to exchange opinions or get advice
words.example: He wanted to confer with his colleagues before reaching a decision.

words.def: a statement that a person makes, admitting that they are guilty of a crime; the act of making such a statement
words.example: After hours of questioning by police, she made a full confession.
